rationale: ADR-0037-amendment lens. (1) The spec characterizes ADR-0037's current model correctly: §3 is verbatim 'N-1, one dissent tolerated' single-homed in ApproveThreshold; §7 grants abandonment as 'legitimate precisely because always audited, never silent'; §8 is 'every gate input is agent-writable, anti-footgun not anti-adversary'. The amendment is coherent: RC blocks like REJECT (§3 threshold becomes necessary-but-not-sufficient floor), a refuted RC clears the veto but never increments Approves so it cannot buy past the floor (keeps ADR-0040's approve_threshold meaningful and honors 'config selects who holds authority, not the evidence'), and the audited refutation is a faithful new instance of the §7 hatch pattern, not a new principle. (2) All four [ADR-XXXX](path) links resolve: ADR-0037/0035/0040 hyphenated files and ADR-0023.md all exist under .mindspec/adr with exact basenames; ../../adr/ resolves correctly from the spec dir. (3) The no-refute-advertising rule mirrors §7's MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL never-print-a-paste-able-prefix discretion (HC-7) precisely and is consistent. (4) No new ADR is warranted: this amends §1/§3/§7 and re-affirms §6/§8; ADR-0040 already covers the layering. ADR-0035 recovery-line and ADR-0040 layering claims are accurate.
findings: MINOR (non-blocking): OQ2 and R2 add a new `refutations` array to the panel.json schema, which is an ADR-0037 §1 (Registration schema) amendment (parallel to how spec 112's `gate` field and spec 109's `approve_threshold` were each documented as §1/§3 schema additions). The ADR Touchpoints bullet for ADR-0037 enumerates only '§3 rule extension + §7 new escape; §6/§8 re-affirmed' and does not name the §1 schema addition. The intent is captured (OQ2 explicitly says 'ADR-0037 §1 schema amendment', R2 gives the exact schema, AC6 greps for 'refut' in the ADR), so an implementer will add it — but the touchpoints/amendment text should list §1 alongside §3/§7 for precedent parity. Not a mischaracterization or dangling link; does not block approval.

OBSERVATION: R1 tightens beyond bare REQUEST_CHANGES — an unrecognized/non-canonical verdict string (today 'neither', so 5 APPROVE + 1 unrecognized passes) now Blocks under 'every latest-round verdict must be APPROVE or refuted-RC' and is explicitly non-refutable. This is a deliberate, documented anti-footgun tightening consistent with ADR-0037's intent, stated openly in R1 and its falsifiers; flagged only so the ADR amendment text notes it, since it slightly exceeds the literal 'RC' framing of the operator decision.

VERIFIED: block-message discretion is airtight vs §7 — the spec forbids printing a paste-able refutation incantation exactly as §7/Alternative-3 forbid printing a paste-able MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL prefix; escape stays discoverable in §7 + /ms-panel-tally. No new ADR needed.
